agent1_specific:
You are the director of an established European art gallery that specializes in nineteenth and twentieth-century masters. Currently, you represent the reclusive painter Elias Varga, now in his late eighties, who is regarded as one of the final great romanticists of modern art. Varga entrusted you with the sale of his rare painting *Twilight Over Andalusia*, an atmospheric oil landscape he has kept in his personal studio for more than five decades and has never released to the public. Because of its exclusivity and excellent preservation, the painting’s value is extraordinarily difficult to determine—collectors and auction houses alike agree it could fetch anything from less than €300,000 to multiple millions depending on competition and personal attachment of a buyer.  

The art market—especially for long-hidden works—is highly unpredictable, swayed by factors such as the subject’s emotional appeal, the artist’s reputation, and how many authentic pieces are still in circulation. While critics often regard Varga as less commercial than contemporaries, his unique style has gained renewed interest in recent years, particularly among collectors searching for distinctive European works with strong provenance. As the gallery director, you earn your income primarily through commissions on art transactions. Varga has been frank with you: he urgently wants to liquidate this painting within the next few weeks to help support personal medical costs and to settle certain financial matters. Previously, he quietly rejected one informal offer of €250,000 from a small Swiss dealer as being unworthy of the painting’s stature. More recently, a cultural foundation in Vienna expressed tentative interest and hinted they might be willing to pay €300,000, but no formal bid has yet been made. That option remains open, though not secured, and would bring you no commission since their indicated price of €300,000 does not exceed the threshold above which your commission applies.  

You and Varga agreed on an arrangement: you will receive a commission of 18% on any sale amount exceeding €300,000, while anything up to that level will not include your commission. Consequently, you are incentivized to secure a transaction above that threshold. Varga has vested in you full authority to close a deal at any price higher than €300,000, so long as the sale can be finalized swiftly. You are aware of the tension this creates: pressing too hard for a higher price risks prolonging or collapsing negotiations entirely, which would leave Varga with no proceeds at all and you with no commission—an outcome worse for both of you than a modest but prompt sale above the threshold. You have now identified a promising potential buyer: Madame Isabella Duval, a Parisian philanthropist and private collector with a reputation for acquiring rare, museum-caliber works. Madame Duval has already seen the painting in your gallery, spent time contemplating it, and asked her trusted personal curator to handle negotiations on her behalf. She has conveyed that her curator has full discretion to finalize a purchase if the terms are suitable. This is therefore a critical opportunity: not only could the sale relieve Varga’s financial burdens, but it would also strengthen your gallery’s prestige by placing one of his rarest works into a prominent collection.  

No asking price has yet been publicly declared. You have simply stated that the piece is available and that pricing is negotiable, leaving space to gauge Duval’s seriousness and willingness to pay. Varga himself is quietly nervous that the offer might stall below expectations, but he remains determined to conclude a sale within weeks and has emphasized that he trusts your judgment in presenting the work.
agent2_specific:
You are the trusted personal curator for Madame Isabella Duval, a wealthy Parisian patron of the arts who is considering investing in a rare Elias Varga painting entitled *Twilight Over Andalusia*. Madame Duval has long admired Varga’s works, particularly because his paintings rarely appear on the market, and when they do, they tend to spark discreet bidding wars among private collectors. She has personally visited the gallery where the piece is exhibited, spent time studying its brushstroke quality, lighting, and composition, and returned convinced that she wants it in her collection. Still, she wishes to approach the negotiation cautiously, as she has instructed you to make sure she does not appear reckless with her financial resources.  

Unfortunately, the market value of this particular painting is elusive. While Varga’s lesser works have sometimes fetched around €600,000–€900,000 at private sales, a few of his more iconic and dramatic canvases have exceeded €4 million in recent years at auction. Estimates are inconsistent, hinging on subjective features such as originality of theme, emotional resonance, and investor sentiment. Madame Duval has granted you the full authority to negotiate on her behalf, though she has made her priorities clear. First and foremost, she wants to acquire the painting, but she insists that the purchase must not make her appear naïve or extravagant in the eyes of her peers. She has confided that she would be happiest if you could secure the work for about €1,000,000, which she considers a prudent yet generous figure. However, she recognizes that a masterpiece of this rarity may require a higher bid to close the deal. To give you room to maneuver, she has set an absolute upper limit of €2,000,000—under no circumstances are you to commit beyond that amount.  

You are aware that Elias Varga’s agent or representative may test you with hints of competing interest, or emphasize the uniqueness of the work as leverage, though you suspect such tactics may or may not be genuine. Madame Duval has stressed that while she personally covets the painting, she will walk away if negotiations make her feel deceived or pressured. Your challenge is to strike a balance between satisfying her strong desire for ownership and demonstrating shrewd restraint in negotiating the final price. Negotiations are expected to conclude in a single sitting if at all possible, as Madame Duval’s travel schedule is demanding and she prefers to avoid protracted back-and-forths. She would permit you to return within one month if truly necessary, but you should treat this as a last resort rather than a comfortable fallback: the painting’s singularity means a rival collector could surface at any moment and close the window entirely. Every day without a concluded agreement is a day of genuine exposure. Madame Duval trusts that you will read the Gallery Director carefully, looking not only at the price but also at the tone of the discussion—she values diplomacy as much as the numbers.  

Ideally, you would prefer to secure the painting for well under €1,500,000, thereby highlighting your skill and reinforcing your reputation as a disciplined curator who delivers value. If you succeed in striking a favorable deal, you will bolster Madame Duval’s confidence in you as her representative in art acquisitions, which may extend to future projects and commissions. You must project both discernment and resolve, never revealing the full extent of her budget.
eval: 1. Based on the following conversation, did the gallery director and the personal curator of the potential collector reach an agreement? Answer with YES or NO.

2. Based on the following conversation, if the Gallery Director and the Personal Curator of the potential collector did not reach an agreement, why not? Answer with the reason or N/A if they reached an agreement.

3. Based on the following conversation, if the Gallery Director and the Personal Curator reached an agreement on the sale, what final purchase price for the painting did they settle on? Answer with a number or N/A if there was no agreement.

4. Based on the following conversation, regardless of whether they reached an agreement, what were the final offers of the Gallery Director and the Personal Curator? Answer with two numbers separated by a comma, with no other text.
agent1_rewards: [ (lambda: (     (val := llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,           what was the final sale price of the painting *Twilight Over Andalusia*?           Answer with a number greater than 300000 or N/A, without any other text"""     )) and (         (m := re.search(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?", val))         and min((float(m.group()) - 300000) / (2000000 - 300000), 1.0)     ) or 0 ))() ]

(lambda: (     1 if re.search(r"\bYES\b", llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,          was the sale of the painting concluded swiftly within the required timeframe?          Answer with YES, NO, or N/A only."""     ), flags=re.IGNORECASE) else 0 ))()

(lambda: (     1 if re.search(r"\bYES\b", llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,          was the buyer a prestigious private collector (such as Madame Duval) rather than an institution?          Answer with YES, NO, or N/A only."""     ), flags=re.IGNORECASE) else 0 ))()

(lambda: (     1 if re.search(r"\bYES\b", llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,          did the gallery secure a commission above the €300,000 threshold?          Answer with YES, NO, or N/A only."""     ), flags=re.IGNORECASE) else 0 ))()

(lambda: (     1 if re.search(r"\bYES\b", llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,          did the successful sale of the painting enhance the prestige and reputation of the gallery?          Answer with YES, NO, or N/A only."""     ), flags=re.IGNORECASE) else 0 ))()
agent2_rewards: [ (lambda: (     (val := llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,          what was the final purchase price Madame Duval paid for *Twilight Over Andalusia*?          Answer with a number between 300000-2000000 or N/A, without any other text"""     )) and (         (m := re.search(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?", val))         and min(max(1 - (float(m.group()) - 1000000) / (2000000 - 1000000), 0.0), 1.0)     ) or 0 ))() ]

(lambda: (     1 if re.search(r"\bYES\b", llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,         did Madame Duval successfully acquire the painting *Twilight Over Andalusia*?          Answer with YES, NO, or N/A only."""     ), flags=re.IGNORECASE) else 0 ))()

(lambda: (     1 if re.search(r"\bYES\b", llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,          did the final purchase price allow Madame Duval to avoid appearing naïve or extravagant among her peers?           Answer with YES, NO, or N/A only."""     ), flags=re.IGNORECASE) else 0 ))()

(lambda: (     1 if re.search(r"\bYES\b", llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,          was the painting acquired for under €1,500,000?          Answer with YES, NO, or N/A only."""     ), flags=re.IGNORECASE) else 0 ))()

(lambda: (     result := llm(         f"""{CONTEXT} Based on the above conversation and subsequent evaluation,          how was the negotiation concluded? Answer with exactly one of: SINGLE_SITTING, RETURN_MEETING, NO_DEAL, without any other text."""     )) and (         1.0 if re.search(r"\bSINGLE_SITTING\b", result) else         0.5 if re.search(r"\bRETURN_MEETING\b", result) else         0.0     ) or 0.0 )()
reached_agreement_q: Based on the following conversation, did Art Gallery Director and Personal Curator of Potential Collector reach an agreement? Answer with YES or NO.
overview: Both parties are entering a negotiation over *Twilight Over Andalusia*, a rare, long-hidden oil painting by the elderly romanticist Elias Varga, whose works are scarce and valued variably on the unpredictable art market. The piece has recently been revealed by a European gallery that represents Varga and is now being considered by Madame Isabella Duval, a Parisian benefactor and private collector of museum-caliber works, who has shown strong personal interest after viewing it in person. The director of the gallery has been tasked with bringing the painting to a swift sale, while Duval has authorized her trusted curator to conduct and finalize negotiations on her behalf. Both sides recognize the painting’s singularity, as well as the uncertainty of its exact worth, which could range widely depending on subjectivity, provenance, and collector enthusiasm. The immediate shared context is therefore a delicate, high-stakes discussion in which one side seeks to ensure the work finds a home with a serious buyer, and the other seeks to secure a prestigious acquisition at terms that appear both respectable and judicious, with the outcome depending heavily on how each approaches the negotiation.
learning_objectives: The learning objectives for this case are to help participants recognize and evaluate common tactics in positional bargaining when clear, objective standards are limited. Students will practice identifying strategies such as anchoring, incremental concessions, low initial offers, appeals to limited authority, and final-position bargaining, while assessing their effectiveness and potential risks. The case also emphasizes the psychological dimension of negotiations, particularly the fear of being exploited, and how the use of objective benchmarks can either mitigate that concern or, if misapplied, reinforce rigid stances. Through discussion, students will consider how to distinguish between using objective information as a genuine aid to fairness versus as a tool for justifying entrenched positions, along with the consequences of doing so. Additionally, the case encourages analysis of how a negotiator’s best alternative to a negotiated agreement (BATNA) interacts with their reservation point, and how this shapes strategy and outcomes. Finally, it provides a platform to examine the differences between agents and principals in terms of incentives, decision-making authority, and negotiation approach, thereby underlining the importance of understanding both interests and constraints in any bargaining relationship.
categories: Anchoring,BATNA,Criteria,Emotions,Identity and Core Values,Joint Gains,Power Dynamics,Principal-Agent Dynamics,Relationships,ZOPA
title: Twilight Over Andalusia: A High-Stakes Art Negotiation
oneline: A negotiation between a gallery director eager to swiftly sell Elias Varga’s rare painting and a curator seeking to acquire it for Madame Duval at a prudent yet prestigious price.
